Philadelphia Area Manufacturers Report Surprise Activity Growth -- Philadelphia Fed
data.description:
By Ed Frankl


Manufacturing activity in the Philadelphia region unexpectedly expanded at the fastest rate in four months in January, helped by rebounding new orders and shipments, a monthly survey said.

The Federal Reserve Bank of Philadelphia said Thursday that its general business activity index for this month climbed to 12.6, the highest reading since September, from an upwardly revised minus 8.8 in December.

A reading above zero indicates an increase in business activity. Economists polled by The Wall Street Journal expected a reading of minus 4.5.

More than 23% of firms surveyed reported rising activity, contrasting with the roughly 11% that saw a decrease, the survey said.

Indexes for new orders and shipments moved significantly higher, while the survey's employment index declined but continues to suggest overall increases in employment, the Philadelphia Fed said. The inventories index fell to its lowest level since July 2024.

Meanwhile, companies continued to report overall increases in prices, though at a marginally slower rate, with price indexes still well above their long-run averages.

However, the survey's indexes for future activity declined, showing the weakest optimism since July, though they continued to suggest overall growth in the next six months, the Philadelphia Fed said.

The data follows the Thursday publication of the New York Fed's gauge of manufacturing, which also improved in January.


Write to Ed Frankl at edward.frankl@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-15-26 0913ET

Fed's Goolsbee tells CNBC getting inflation lower is Fed's main job
data.description:
Jan 15 (Reuters) - Federal Reserve Bank of Chicago President Austan Goolsbee said Thursday that amid ample evidence of stability in the job market the central bank should be focused on getting inflation down, in comments that kept alive the prospect of rate cuts later this year.

"There's still strength" in the job market and "I think the most important thing facing us is we've got to get inflation back to 2%," Goolsbee said in an interview on CNBC. "Rates can go down still a fair amount" with cuts even happening this year, but "we have to have convincing evidence that we're on path back to 2% inflation."

(Reporting by Michael S. Derby; )

U.S. Import Prices Rise
data.description:
By Don Nico Forbes


U.S. import prices rose over the two months to November, Labor Department data showed Thursday.

Import prices rose 0.4% over the two months, the data showed. Year on year, November import prices rose 0.1%.

Import prices exclude duties, such as tariffs imposed on imports by the Trump administration, as well as transportation costs.

Fuel import prices fell in the period, prices for nonfuel imports rose, the Labor Department said.

Data for October and November were originally scheduled to be published on Nov. 18 and Dec. 16, respectively, but faced delays due to the government shutdown.


Write to Don Nico Forbes at don.forbes@wsj.com


(END) Dow Jones Newswires

01-15-26 0907ET

SiFive to adopt Nvidia technology for speedy links between chips
data.description:
SAN FRANCISCO, Jan 15 Reuters) - SiFive, which makes blueprints for parts of complex chips, said on Thursday it will become the first maker of RISC-V chip designs to integrate Nvidia technology that creates speedy links between chips.

Like SoftBank's Arm Holdings - an industry giant, SiFive provides customers with blueprints so that they can build their own full chip designs. RISC-V is an open-standard alternative to Arm's blueprints that has drawn fresh interest from companies like Alphabet's Google and Meta as more details have emerged about Arm's ambitions to become a chip designer in its own right.

Nvidia's NVLink creates speedy links between central processing units (CPUs) and Nvidia's AI chips. Those links are essential in AI data centers where thousands of chips must be strung together to process huge amounts of data seamlessly.

SiFive CEO Patrick Little said SiFive chip designs with the technology will not likely hit the market until 2027 or later. But when they do, customers will be able to connect a RISC-V CPU to Nvidia's leading AI chips with the same speeds as CPUs that use Intel or Arm technology.

"This is a multi-generational commitment from both companies to get to work on this and offer the best NVLink solution, generation after generation after generation," he told Reuters in an interview. 

Nvidia and SiFive did not disclose financial terms of the deal. 

(Reporting by Stephen Nellis in San Francisco; Editing by Edwina Gibbs)
